factors.summary: The global economy is experiencing a rapid slowdown due to the COVID-19 pandemic, which has negatively impacted trade and demand for services.

evidence.text: The global economy is experiencing a rapid slowdown as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, which has affected trade and could further affect demand for our services.

factors.summary: Demand trends are decelerating due to reduced freight volumes and lower customer activity caused by the pandemic.

evidence.text: COVID-19 significantly impacted worldwide economic conditions and global trade and may continue to have an adverse effect on our operations, and the operations of our service providers and our customers, which may further impact our business.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining due to reduced flight schedules and cancellations affecting airfreight and ocean carriers.

evidence.text: Reduced flight schedules and cancellations have significantly reduced available space for airfreight, while ocean carriers have continued to reduce their operating capacity.

factors.summary: Pricing power is being challenged due to market conditions causing volatility and difficulty in maintaining historical profitability.

evidence.text: These freight market conditions create pricing volatility that challenges Expeditors’ ability to maintain historical unitary profitability.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is growing due to increased accounts receivable collection risks from customers experiencing financial instability.

evidence.text: Many of our customers are experiencing disruptions in their revenue and cash flow, prompting these customers to renegotiate contractual terms and increasing our accounts receivable collection risks. Such conditions could result in the loss of business and additional bad debt allowances in the future if our customers’ ability to pay deteriorates.

factors.summary: Organic growth is decelerating due to economic slowdown and disruptions in supply chains caused by the pandemic.

evidence.text: The global economy is experiencing a rapid slowdown as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, which has affected trade and could further affect demand for our services. Continued unfavorable economic conditions will result in lower freight volumes and adversely affects Expeditors' revenues, operating results and cash flows.

factors.summary: Cost actions are being implemented to manage the disruptions caused by the pandemic, but the effectiveness is uncertain.

evidence.text: Although we are monitoring the situation, we cannot predict for how long, or the ultimate extent to which the pandemic and related precautionary measures may disrupt our operations. Any significant disruption resulting from this on a large scale or over an extended period of time would negatively affect our business and our financial results.

factors.summary: The supply chain is still disrupted due to reduced carrier capacity, pricing volatility, and limited transportation schedules.

evidence.text: COVID-19 is expected to place significant stress on our air, ocean and freight ground carriers, which may result in reduced carrier capacity or availability, pricing volatility or more limited carrier transportation schedules which could adversely impact our operations and financial results.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting negatively due to lower freight volumes and reduced demand in certain segments.

evidence.text: The global economy is experiencing a rapid slowdown as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, which has affected trade and could further affect demand for our services. Continued unfavorable economic conditions will result in lower freight volumes and adversely affects Expeditors' revenues, operating results and cash flows.

factors.summary: Input costs are being affected by reduced carrier capacity and increased regulations, which may lead to higher costs.

evidence.text: Additionally, Expeditors' carriers are subject to increasingly stringent laws, which could, directly or indirectly, have a material adverse effect on our business. Future regulatory developments in the U.S. and abroad could adversely affect operations and increase operating costs in the transportation industry, which in turn could increase our purchased transportation costs.
